description:
"I wish someone would make a mod."



Garry:

Main.description:
Uses [Inititial Release Version of GMAP](https://github.com/ChrisCj8/gm_ap/releases/tag/Main)

Initial Release

If you don't know what the .fgd file is for you don't need it.
Main.title: Initial Release

v0.1.2.description:
Uses [Version 0.1.1 of GMAP](https://github.com/ChrisCj8/gm_ap/releases/tag/v0.1.1)

- adopted a new versioning scheme
- gmod path is now set in a text file separate from host.yaml since accessing that during datapackage creation is too unreliable
    - if the path was set in host.yaml beforehand it will be migrated automatically for now
- edit mode tools will now stop you from creating locations, exits, entrances and start point with invalid settings
- added movement values from Half-Life: Source to the editor presets
- users are now prevented from saving over built-in configs unless the convar `apadventure_editor_allow_static_overwrite` is set to 1 to prevent people from accidentally overwriting stuff they shouldn't
- issues encountered by the apworld when processing configs will now be logged into a file in the gmod_apadv folder
- starts and entrances now have player-sized bounding boxes drawn around them in edit mode
- fixed the start tool not creating toolgun effects when used
- fixed a bunch of ui files not being addcsluafile'd
- made some minor changes to the documentation

if you don't know what the .fgd file is for you don't need it
v0.1.2.title: Version 0.1.2

v0.2.0.description:
Uses [Version 0.1.1 of GMAP](https://github.com/ChrisCj8/gm_ap/releases/tag/v0.1.1)

new features/changes:
- added two new launcher options that can generate text files containing information about all loaded item sets and map configs
- added a new requirement system that tells players what content they need to have installed/enabled for their run and lets them create a preset to help automatically download missing workshop addons
- added map configs for the Half-Life: Source version of Unforeseen Consequences (no HL1 weapons are included atm because the ones GMod includes by default are very broken)
- players will now always start their runs with a melee weapon if their chosen item sets contain a weapon marked as a starting melee
- added a yaml option that lets players choose to get additional starting items from other starting item pools
- added yaml options that let players choose which starting point they want to use for their runs
- entrance and start point entities in map config edit mode now have their own models
- the gamemode will now show a warning to the player if they try to enter a map that is not mounted
- trying to save a config with no entrances or a start point but no exits will now send a warning to the player
- the error thrown if the generator cannot place all entrance will now display additional information
- added new implied capabilities
- adjusted capabilities for most hl2 weapons

fixes:
- fixed item definitions from the gmod folder not being loaded properly if there are conflicts
- map items for maps that could not be added to the run because they would've been inaccessible with the current yaml setting will no longer be added to the run
- stopped the permission hook system from throwing errors if the permission function is undefined in sandbox
- cfg processor now only processes files in the item folders if they end in .json
- reworked the map and group tabs in the editor to use the dock system instead of PerformLayout to place the elements for different options since that was a little broken + other minor ui code improvements
- fixed configs with too many entrances potentially causing an endless loop during entrance randomization
- fixed one-way entrances not having access rules applied to them
- improved preprocessing for access rules, which should make generation faster and also prevent locations/region that could never be accessed with the current yaml settings from being added to the run

if you don't know what the .fgd file is for, you don't need it
v0.2.0.title: Version 0.2.0

v0.3.0.description:
Uses [Version 0.2.0 of GMAP](https://github.com/ChrisCj8/gm_ap/releases/tag/v.0.2.0)

Changes/Additions:
- added configs for some bhop maps by pcpie and phaze and a yaml template featuring them
- added a config for lost coast and added it to the yaml defaults
- the unduck speed for the player class in now set to be instant, so players no longer their momentum when unducking while bhopping
- the bhop_logic yaml option now takes a float between 0 and 10 and the bhop node can now have a skill rating set on it to allow bhop logic to account for different skill levels
- added a new surf_logic node and yaml option, it functions similarly to the new bhop node/option but without an item requirement
- d1_town_01a is now considered to be traversable in reverse if surf logic is set to at least 0.5
- added the min access node, which checks if at least x amount of the access rules under it are considered accessible
- config scripts can now contain two tables of functions called ItemFuncs and MapItemFuncs which can contain functions that will be run after the map is first loaded and whenever the player receives items with names matching the table keys + some improvements to config script loading and RegisterMapItems
- weapon pickups are no longer suppressed while `sv_cheats` is set 1 to make testing configs easier
- the config loading menu was reworked into a file manager menu which also allow configs to be copied and deleted
- added `APADV_FULLCONNECT` global to the gamemode for a quick way to check if the game is fully connected to AP
- reworked the way the misc requirements work and added a way load custom code for displaying them
- added new requirement tags that can check what wiremod e2 extensions are enabled
- the godmode config rule now also disables fall damage (it already prevented the actual damage, but not the screenshake and sound effects)
  - this may be reverted at some point when i add a better way of adjusting fall damage behaviour
- the editor will now show a warning if the player tried to save a config without any regions
- map icon materials for maps that don't have icons now show the maps name
- the resolution for map icon materials can now be adjusted through the `apadventure_mapiconmat_resolution` convar
- added a "Desired Tickrate" rule that can be set on a config to have the gamemode request the player to change their tickrate (this not something that can be adjusted while the game is running so this can't be done automatically)
- the help text for the capab node now tells users to use the item index instead of linking to the item definition files
- made the warning shown when the processor can't find (part of) a config more descriptive
- the entrance randomizer will now raise a warning early if no exits are reachable from the selected start position and there are more entrances to connect
- the error thrown when the randomizer can't make enough space to fit all progression items is now more informative
- the warning thrown saving a config without entrances will now more explicitly state that the config won't work
- added a note about unreachable regions being discarded to the region tabs help drawer
- the name and description for the config option that handles respawning was adjusted

Fixes/Code Changes:
- fixed the Start Point Tool not playing Toolgun animations and sounds when using the reload function
- changed how the config script for d2_prison_07 detects that all waves in the cell block have been cleared because someone reported that it didn't trigger for them
- fixed the hints telling people that they don't have to clear the waves again in d2_prison_07 not using localization strings
- access node functions now run on the scroll panel canvases 
- fixed the group name not being set clientside when saving a config
- fixed save and reload options in the editor menu not being enabled after saving a new config
- fixed the tracker not handling "and" and "or"-nodes properly
- some localization strings for the node edit panels are now shared
- fixed and and or nodes breaking the tracker when one of their subnodes needs to be removed
- fixed the tracker sending clients the region an exit is in instead of its name when initially building the tracking table
- removed unnecessary slot data checks from the bhop node
- fixed the .fgd not working in regular hammer
- fixed lua errors stemming from a table used to log which players still need to have their save data applied to them not being reset properly
- fixed the tracker not rechecking location accessiblity unless the entire region they're in needs to be rechecked
- slot data is now handled by GMAP instead of the gamemode itself
- save data folders are now named using slot and team ids instead of slot names, which could cause problems if the the players slot name was not filesystem-friendly
- fixed ui elements in the pop-up menu for adding workshop requirements not using localization strings or lacking text entirely
- fixed new requirements added after loading a config/reopening the editor window replacing existing requirements
- moved some localization strings relating to requirement tags to apadventure_requiretags since having them there makes more sense
- fixed a bug with the capability node edit panel that would cause the capability list to be larger than it's supposed to be
- fixed warnings in cfgprocessor_warnings.log not having line breaks inbetween them
- the right click menus some ui elements have now use DermaMenu and DMenu:Open instead of vgui.Create and SetPos
- fixed the mcguffin count being capped by the amount of items in the item pool rather than the remaining space
- fixed the schema for the cherrypicking/blacklisting options allowing users to enter a single string rather than a list despite that never being supported
- items added in item_cherrypick are now removed if they're blacklisted
  - i considered just leaving this as is since you shouldn't be having the same item in the blacklist and cherrypick option anyways but the config counterparts of these options already behave like this so i changed it for consistency
- fixed lua errors being thrown if a player uses an exit right after the config (re)loads
- fixed some warnings from the config processor only being printed instead of being output to the warning log
- fixed a typo in one of the error messages that could be raised during entrance randomization and replaced "Player" with "slot"
- fixed regions that are determined to be inaccesible still being added as start regions

if you don't know what the .fgd file is for, you don't need it
v0.3.0.title: Version 0.3.0

v1_1.description:
Uses [Inititial Release Version of GMAP](https://github.com/ChrisCj8/gm_ap/releases/tag/Main)

Fixed the apworld throwing errors on new installs because the host.yaml section and logic folders haven't been created yet.

This flew under my radar because I did not test the apworld on a new install, apologies.

If you don't know what the .fgd file is for you don't need it.
v1_1.title: Version 1 Patch 1
